title: Keyboard navigation
question: Can you move through the explorer with the keyboard, not just the mouse?
rationale: Keyboard support: up/down move between decisions, '/' focuses search, Esc clears, Enter opens the top result, reusing the select path so the hash and scroll follow. Keys live behind a discreet '?' overlay rather than an always-on hint bar, keeping the calm one-focus design (d2, d9).

options.label: Arrow-key nav with a quiet help button

tradeoffs.note: no permanent on-screen hints; the '?' overlay appears only when asked
the list is fully navigable without a mouse
binds keys to the existing select path; no new rendering
a quiet '?' button (top-right) and Cmd+? reveal the shortcuts

options.label: Keyboard nav with an always-visible hint bar

tradeoffs.note: the shortcuts are always in view
a permanent hint bar clutters the calm layout d2/d9 chose
slower to move through, and unusable without a mouse

history.reason: Added a visible '?' button in the top-right and moved the trigger to Cmd+? for discoverability, keeping the calm layout (no always-on hint bar).
